title: Notholaena delavayi
taxonomy: Notholaena delavayi is classified as a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. This genus and family are part of the larger group of plants known as vascular plants. The classification of Notholaena delavayi is further detailed within the kingdom Plantae. The species is native to the mountainous regions of China, where it grows in shaded, humid environments. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other fern species within the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Notholaena delavayi, also known as Delavay's fern or Chinese rock fern, is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of N. delavayi include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, N. delavayi is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive disorders.
